text.title: Pricing Experiment Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every Stripe-based pricing experiment must satisfy. Hard rules; designed to prevent the four standard incidents: re-billed grandfathered customers, refund storms, currency-tax errors, and tests that never statistically close.

rule.statement: The Experiment Plan is written and committed to the marketing repo BEFORE any Stripe change is made. It states: variant (old vs new price + structure), audience (new sign-ups only / existing customers / segment), exposure rule (random 50/50, geo split, cohort), duration, success metric with significance threshold, and a defined termination rule.

rationale.source: A/B-testing methodology; pre-registration in clinical trials

rule.rationale: Without pre-commit, the founder consciously or unconsciously picks the success metric that already won. Pre-registered design also makes the "this test failed" outcome cheaper to accept, because the bar was set before the data was in.

rule.statement: Existing customers are grandfathered explicitly: either kept on the old Stripe Price object indefinitely, or moved with a documented coupon stack that holds the old effective price for a defined window. The grandfathering rule is written in the Experiment Plan and announced to customers in writing. The old Stripe Price object is NEVER deleted.

rationale.source: customer-retention research on price-change perception; Stripe-billing incident postmortems

rule.rationale: Accidentally re-billing a grandfathered customer at a higher price is the single most-cited "I am cancelling and leaving a bad review" event. Deleting the old Price object is the second most-cited; Stripe will silently re-bill the customer at the new price on the next renewal. The discipline is "old Price stays in Stripe forever, even if archived in the dashboard".

example.title: Grandfathering with coupon stack

rule.statement: Before the experiment goes live, run `scripts/stripe-pre-flight.py` against the production Stripe account. It verifies: new Price object exists with the experiment variant, old Price object active, grandfather coupon attached to all eligible customers, no orphaned subscriptions, no test-mode artifacts in live. Test cannot start until pre-flight returns green.

rationale.source: Stripe Dashboard error patterns; production-deploy gating practice

rule.rationale: Manual checklist execution misses one in ten verifications. A scripted pre-flight is the only mechanism that prevents the "I thought I checked that" failure mode. Stripe-specific errors (currency mismatch on Price object, deleted Plan referenced by active subscription) are not visible from the Dashboard alone.

rule.statement: For every Price object in the experiment, currency, tax behavior (`tax_behavior: inclusive | exclusive`), and tax-id capture flow are documented in the Plan. Multi-currency experiments require a separate Price object per currency and an explicit display-rule (visitor's IP-geo or browser-locale). EU VAT, UK VAT, GST, and US state-sales-tax are handled at announcement time, not after the first invoice rejects.

rationale.source: Stripe Tax documentation; cross-border SaaS invoicing incidents

rule.rationale: Currency conversion at billing time silently changes the perceived price by 3-7% in either direction, breaking the experiment's framing. Tax-inclusive vs tax-exclusive defaults differ by jurisdiction; mismatched tax behavior surfaces as a customer-support storm 14 days into the experiment.

rule.statement: On the final scheduled day of the experiment, the founder writes the Decision Memo: variant-level metrics, significance verdict, qualitative signals (support tickets, customer feedback, refund rate), and a single binary decision (adopt new price / revert to old / extend test with documented adjustment). The memo is committed to the marketing repo within 48h of the experiment end. No "let's leave it running and see" is permitted.

rationale.source: decision-discipline patterns; sunk-cost research in experiment governance

rule.rationale: Experiments that drift past their planned duration become indistinguishable from the new status quo, and the founder loses the ability to revert. Forced go/no-go at the planned date preserves the experiment as a real choice.

list.item: Refund rate > 2x baseline OR support-ticket rate > 3x baseline — terminate immediately, revert to old Price
Stripe dispute rate spikes > 0.5% during the window — terminate immediately, revert
Significance not reached but sample size at 80% of plan — extend by 30 days max, then force go/no-go
Significance reached with negative effect on MRR or trial-to-paid — terminate, revert, document
